description: Port FeynCalc Feynman/ directory: FeynRule (extract Feynman rule from Lagrangian term), propagator functions, vertex constructors, coupling constants. These bridge Phase 3 (model) and Phase 4 (diagrams). Ref: Feynman/ directory (32 files, 6604 lines).
title: Cross-cutting: Feynman rules (FeynRule, propagators, vertices)

description: Implement scalar ULDM portal Lagrangian, extract coupling constants (d_{m_e}, d_e), compute 1-loop radiative corrections, connect to VLBAI pipeline. This is the end-to-end integration test for the full stack. PRD §9.2 milestone.
title: Phase 6: ULDM Application (end-to-end integration)

description: Define L_φ = φ√(4πG_N) [d_e/(4e²) F_μν F^μν − d_{m_e} m_e ψ̄_e ψ_e] as a Feynfeld model. Extract Feynman rules from this Lagrangian using Phase 3 machinery.
title: Implement ULDM scalar portal Lagrangian

description: Extract d_{m_e} and d_e coupling constants from the scalar portal Lagrangian. Compute 1-loop radiative corrections. Produce numerical coupling constants for VLBAI signal pipeline input.
title: Compute ULDM coupling constants at tree level and 1-loop

description: PRD §9.3 community milestone: reproduce AION-10/100/km scalar ULDM sensitivity projections from Badurina et al., matching published figures to plotting accuracy. End-to-end validation of the full Feynfeld stack.
title: Reproduce Badurina et al. (2109.10965) sensitivity projections

description: Port FeynCalc's Lorentz algebra core: Pair, Momentum, ScalarProduct, ExpandScalarProduct, MomentumExpand/Combine, Contract, Eps. This is the foundation all other algebra builds on.
title: Phase 1a: Lorentz Algebra (Pair/Contract)

close_reason: Design spike complete: hybrid approach chosen (universal Pair type from FeynCalc + SPContext from Julia-idiomatic design + fold/reduce contract). Three agents ran: research, Design A, Design B.
description: FeynCalc's Pair[x,y] encodes 3 things via argument heads: metric (LI,LI), four-vector (LI,Mom), scalar product (Mom,Mom). In Julia: decide whether to use one Pair type with canonical ordering + inner constructor (like FeynCalc) or separate types. Must handle Orderless (canonical arg ordering). Must integrate with TensorGR's TProduct. Research FeynCalc SharedObjects.m lines 1-300.
title: Design Pair type system for Julia

close_reason: Momentum type implemented in src/algebra/momentum.jl with DimSlot support, ordering, hash. 14 tests.
description: Port FeynCalc Momentum. Key rules: Momentum[0,_]=0, Momentum[n*x,dim]=n*Momentum[x,dim], Momentum[x,4]=Momentum[x]. Must support 4/D/(D-4) dimension slots. Implement MomentumExpand (Momentum[a+b]→Momentum[a]+Momentum[b]) and MomentumCombine (inverse). Ref: SharedObjects.m, Lorentz/MomentumExpand.m, MomentumCombine.m.
title: Implement Momentum type with linearity and dimension tagging

close_reason: ScalarProduct mechanism implemented: Pair(Momentum,Momentum) replaces old ScalarProduct struct, SPContext for value storage, SP/SPD/SPE convenience constructors. 33 tests for Pair + 10 for SPContext.
description: Port FeynCalc's ScalarProduct[p,q]=m² mechanism. In FeynCalc this adds DownValues to Pair. In Julia: a registry/dict mapping (p,q)→value, applied during simplification. Must support SetDimensions (define SP in multiple dimension slots simultaneously). Ref: Lorentz/ScalarProduct.m.
title: Implement ScalarProduct definition mechanism

close_reason: ExpandScalarProduct implemented in expand_sp.jl. MomentumSum added to momentum.jl with full arithmetic. Bilinear expansion of Pair(MomentumSum,x), SPContext integration. 39 new tests.
description: Port FeynCalc ExpandScalarProduct: expand Pair[Momentum[a+b], Momentum[c]] → Pair[Momentum[a],Momentum[c]] + Pair[Momentum[b],Momentum[c]]. Uses linearity, NOT Mathematica's Expand. Finds tensor objects containing Plus and expands individually. Ref: Lorentz/ExpandScalarProduct.m (important: does NOT use Mathematica Expand).
title: Implement ExpandScalarProduct

close_reason: Contract implemented in src/algebra/contract.jl: worklist reducer for metric-metric, metric-vector, vector-vector contractions. Full BMHV coverage (all 9x3 dim combinations). SPContext integration. Einstein violation detection. 46 contract tests.
description: Port FeynCalc Contract. THE core operation. Algorithm: FCSplit to separate indexed/free parts → DummyIndexFreeQ to filter → tree-walk to find repeated indices → substitute. In FeynCalc uses PairContract trick (UpValues for eager contraction). In Julia: explicit tree-walking pass via TensorGR's contract_metrics or a dedicated contract() function. Must handle: metric contraction g^μν g_νρ, four-vector contraction p^μ g_μν, BMHV mixed-dim contractions. Ref: Lorentz/Contract.m (718 lines), PairContract.m (427 lines).
title: Implement Contract (Lorentz index contraction)

close_reason: Eps and EpsContract implemented in eps.jl. Levi-Civita tensor with BMHV vanishing, antisymmetry checks, LC/LCD convenience. EpsContract for 2,3,4 shared indices (4D and D-dim). 17 tests.
description: Port FeynCalc Eps[LorentzIndex[a],...,LorentzIndex[d]] and the contraction rules ε^{μνρσ}ε_{μνρσ}=-24, ε^{μνρσ}ε_{μνρτ}=-6δ^σ_τ, etc. Must handle mixed momentum/index arguments. Must respect $LeviCivitaSign convention. Ref: Lorentz/Eps.m, EpsEvaluate.m, EpsContract.m.
title: Implement Eps (Levi-Civita tensor) and EpsContract

close_reason: Phase 1a validated: 78 MUnit tests translated from PairContract.test, Contract.test, ExpandScalarProduct.test, ScalarProduct.test. 284 total tests pass. Found and fixed expand_scalar_product single-term MomentumSum bug.
description: Translate FeynCalc Tests/Lorentz/*.test files to Julia @test. Priority files: Contract.test (119 tests), ExpandScalarProduct.test, MomentumExpand.test, MomentumCombine.test, Eps.test, EpsEvaluate.test. Preserve mathematical content exactly per MUnit translation protocol.
title: Validate Phase 1a: translate Lorentz MUnit tests

description: Port LoopTools' numerical evaluation of scalar 1-loop integrals to native Julia. Dual-implementation architecture (FF + Denner) for cross-validation. Atomic building block is the dilogarithm Li2. Must match LoopTools to machine precision.
title: Phase 2: Numerical Loop Integrals (LoopTools port)

description: Port LoopTools ffxli2.F (real) and ffcli2.F (complex). The atomic building block — all C0/D0 integrals reduce to Li2 combinations. Uses Bernoulli-number series expansion, careful branch-cut handling, eta function for complex arguments. Also: Li2omx (Li2(1-x) for accuracy near x=1), Spence function, auxCD utilities. Ref: src/util/ffxli2.F, ffcli2.F, Li2.F, Li2omx.F, auxCD.F.
title: Implement dilogarithm Li2 (real and complex)

description: Trivial exact result: A0(m) = m*(1 - log(m/μ²) + δ) where δ = 2/(4-D) - γ_E + log(4π) in MSbar. Also A00. Ref: src/A/Aget.F, ffxa0.F.
title: Implement A0 (scalar 1-point integral)

description: THE MOST ALGORITHMIC 2-point function. ~600 lines of case analysis: both masses zero, one zero, both equal, general case. Each with Taylor expansions for degenerate limits. Threshold/pseudo-threshold handling. Dual implementation (FF + Denner). Also B1, B00, B11, DB0 derivatives. Store as (finite, 1/ε, 1/ε²) triples. Ref: src/B/ffxb0.F (real), ffcb0.F (complex), Bcoeff.F, BcoeffFF.F, BcoeffAD.F.
title: Implement B0 and B-coefficients (scalar 2-point)

description: C0 via van Oldenborgh R-function decomposition + Denner Spence function formula. Dispatch: soft singularities, collinear singularities, 0/1/2/3 zero momenta → specialized routines. PV reduction for tensor coefficients C1..C2222. Gram determinant handling. Ref: src/C/ffxc0.F (~500 lines), C0func.F, Cget.F.
title: Implement C0 and C-coefficients (scalar 3-point)

description: Most complex: checks 12 permutations for soft/collinear, all-zero momenta special case, primary FF decomposition into S4→R→Li2 functions, Denner fallback. 240 tensor coefficients (80 × 3 eps-components). Ref: src/D/D0func.F (~1200 lines), ffxd0.F (~500 lines), Dget.F.
title: Implement D0 and D-coefficients (scalar 4-point)

description: E0 reduces to 5 D0 integrals. 258 tensor coefficients (86 × 3). Ref: src/E/E0func.F, Eget.F, Ecoeffa.F (~1500 lines), ffxe0.F.
title: Implement E0 and E-coefficients (scalar 5-point)

description: Port LoopTools cache: binary tree cache with thread-safe lookup. Avoids recomputation of integrals with same kinematic arguments. In Julia: Dict-based with hash on kinematic args. Ref: src/util/cache.c.
title: Implement integral cache system

description: Port LoopTools COMPLEXPARA system for unstable particles with Breit-Wigner widths. Complex versions of all integral functions. nschem variable controls scheme. Ref: all ffcXX.F files.
title: Implement complex mass scheme

description: Per PRD: call Fortran LoopTools via ccall for comparison. For each integral function, test against LoopTools at multiple kinematic points including edge cases (thresholds, zero masses, equal masses, Gram determinant = 0). Must agree to machine precision. Build LoopTools as shared library.
title: Validate Phase 2: cross-validate against LoopTools via ccall

description: Reimplement FormCalc's functionality natively in Julia: amplitude squaring |M|², spin summation (completeness relations), colour summation, polarization sums, phase-space integration, cross-section computation. FormCalc is NOT ported — its logic is reimplemented.
title: Phase 5: Evaluate (amplitude squaring, cross-sections)

description: Port FeynCalc FermionSpinSum. Applies completeness relations: Σ_s u(p,s)ū(p,s) = p̸+m, Σ_s v(p,s)v̄(p,s) = p̸-m. Converts spin-summed amplitude products into Dirac traces. Ref: Feynman/FermionSpinSum.m.
title: Implement FermionSpinSum (spin-averaged |M|²)

description: Port FeynCalc PolarizationSum. Massless: Σ ε^μ ε*^ν → -g^{μν} + gauge terms. Massive: Σ ε^μ ε*^ν → -g^{μν} + k^μk^ν/m². Ref: Feynman/PolarizationSum.m, FormCalc PolarizationSum.frm.
title: Implement PolarizationSum (gauge boson polarization sums)

description: Reimplement FormCalc SquaredME logic. Separate squared amplitude into form factors and matrix elements. For fermions: M = Σ_i a_i F_i → |M|² = Σ_{ij} a_i* a_j Mat[F_i,F_j]. FormCalc's FF/FFC/Mat separation is the key pattern. Ref: FormCalc.m SquaredME section (~line 5031).
title: Implement amplitude squaring (SquaredME)

description: Compute colour structure of squared amplitudes using exact SU(N) algebra. Product of colour factors summed over colour indices. Uses SUNSimplify/SUNTrace from Phase 1c. Ref: FormCalc.m ColourME section.
title: Implement ColourME (colour matrix elements)

description: 2→2 phase space integration for cross-sections. dσ/dΩ = |M|²/(64π²s). Differential and total cross-sections. Decay rate Γ = |M|²/(16πM). Use Julia integration libraries (Cuba.jl or QuadGK.jl). Ref: Peskin & Schroeder chapter 4-5.
title: Implement phase-space integration and cross-section computation

description: THE MINIMUM VIABLE SUCCESS CRITERION (PRD §9.1). Compute tree-level σ(e+e-→μ+μ-) in QED from the QED Lagrangian and verify it equals Peskin & Schroeder equation (5.10): σ = 4πα²/(3s). Must agree to machine precision. This is the end-to-end integration test for Phases 0-5.
title: Validate Phase 5: e+e-→μ+μ- tree-level cross-section = P&S eq (5.10)

description: Port FeynCalc's PaVe coefficient function symbols, FeynAmpDenominator propagator types, TID (tensor integral decomposition), Tdec (tensor decomposition), PaVeReduce. These are the symbolic loop integral layer — numerical evaluation comes in Phase 2.
title: Phase 1d: PaVe Symbols and Tensor Decomposition

description: Refine current PaVe types. FeynCalc PaVe[i,j,...,{p²},{m²}] with index symmetry and auto-reduction to named scalars. GenPaVe uses different momentum convention. Ref: LoopIntegrals/PaVe.m, SharedObjects.m PaVe section.
title: Implement PaVe type system (A0, B0, B1, C0, D0, GenPaVe)

description: Port FeynCalc FeynAmpDenominator wrapping propagator denominators. PropagatorDenominator[Momentum[q],m] = 1/(q²-m²). StandardPropagatorDenominator (modern form). CartesianPropagatorDenominator. These are symbolic — no numerical evaluation yet. Ref: SharedObjects.m FeynAmpDenominator section.
title: Implement FeynAmpDenominator and propagator types

description: Port FeynCalc TID. Takes TID[amp, q] where q is loop momentum. Decomposes tensor loop integrals into scalar integrals via Passarino-Veltman decomposition. Solves linear system for coefficients. UsePaVeBasis option. Handles vanishing Gram determinants. Ref: LoopIntegrals/TID.m.
title: Implement TID (tensor integral decomposition)

description: Port FeynCalc Tdec. General tensor decomposition using projection methods. Uses Alexey Pak's symmetrization algorithm. TIDL lookup for pre-computed results. Ref: LoopIntegrals/Tdec.m, Tables/TIDL.m.
title: Implement Tdec (tensor decomposition)

description: Port FeynCalc PaVeReduce. Reduces PaVe coefficient functions to scalar A0, B0, C0, D0. Options: BReduce, A0ToB0, $LimitTo4. Ref: LoopIntegrals/PaVeReduce.m.
title: Implement PaVeReduce

description: Translate relevant FeynCalc Tests/LoopIntegrals/*.test for symbolic PaVe operations. 122 test files total — prioritise PaVe.test, PaVeReduce.test, TID.test, Tdec.test.
title: Validate Phase 1d: translate LoopIntegrals MUnit tests (symbolic)

description: Port FeynArts' topology generation, three-level field insertion, and amplitude construction. Core physics ~2800 lines of Mathematica. Recursive topology algorithm + Generic→Classes→Particles hierarchy.
title: Phase 4: Diagrams (FeynArts port)

description: Port FeynArts data structures. Topology[symmetry_factor][Propagator[type][from,to],...]. Vertex[valence][id] with negative id = permutable. Propagator types: Incoming, Outgoing, Internal, Loop[n]. TopologyList as flat collection. Ref: FeynArts/Topology.m data structure section.
title: Implement topology data structures (Topology, Propagator, Vertex, TopologyList)

description: Port FeynArts CreateTopologies. Recursive algorithm: seeds from hardcoded starting topologies per loop order → iteratively adds external legs by splitting propagators or increasing vertex valence. Isomorphism detection via canonical ordering + permutation of permutable vertices. Filter system using ToTree→Centre pattern (tadpoles=Centre[1], self-energies=Centre[2], etc). Ref: FeynArts/Topology.m (843 lines).
title: Implement CreateTopologies (recursive topology generation)

description: Port FeynArts model initialization. GenericModel (.gen): M$GenericPropagators, M$GenericCouplings, M$FlippingRules, KinematicIndices. ClassesModel (.mod): M$ClassesDescription, M$CouplingMatrices. InitializeModel reads these. Must interface with Phase 3's model system. Ref: FeynArts/Initialize.m (1311 lines), Models/Lorentz.gen, Models/SM.mod.
title: Implement three-level model system (.gen/.mod files)

description: Port FeynArts InsertFields. Three-pass: Generic→Classes→Particles. At each level: enumerate valid field assignments, check field point existence, quantum number conservation (ViolatesQ), InsertOnly restrictions, IndexDelta diagonal constraints. Insertion isomorphism detection via Compare. Ref: FeynArts/Insert.m (518 lines).
title: Implement InsertFields (three-pass field insertion)

description: Port FeynArts CreateFeynAmp. Translates inserted topologies into analytical expressions: looks up Feynman rules from model, builds fermion chains, enforces momentum conservation, assembles propagator denominators. Output: amplitude expressions compatible with Algebra layer. Ref: FeynArts/Analytic.m (915 lines).
title: Implement CreateFeynAmp (amplitude construction)

description: Per PRD: validate against FeynArts example calculations. Generate all topologies and insert fields for e+e-→μ+μ- at tree level (1 diagram) and 1-loop (~10 diagrams). Compare topology count and amplitude structure against FeynArts output.
title: Validate Phase 4: e+e- → ff̄ at tree level and 1-loop

description: Extend TensorGR.jl for dimensional regularisation and register Minkowski metric. Foundation for all Lorentz algebra.
title: Phase 0: TensorGR.jl Bridge

close_reason: Changed ManifoldProperties.dim and VBundleProperties.dim from Int to Union{Int,Symbol}. Guarded metric.jl (epsilon tensor, lorentzian fallback), contraction.jl (metric trace), full_simplify.jl (DDI order). All 375,398 TensorGR tests pass.
description: TensorGR.jl's ManifoldProperties has dim::Int. Feynfeld needs dim::Union{Int,Symbol} for D-dimensional regularisation (D = 4-2ε). Modify TensorGR.jl to accept symbolic dimensions. Must not break existing TensorGR tests.
title: Extend TensorGR ManifoldProperties.dim to Union{Int,Symbol}

close_reason: minkowski_registry() implemented in src/algebra/minkowski.jl with 12 passing tests
description: Create a Feynfeld-side registry setup that registers a 4D Minkowski manifold with lorentzian(4) signature and flat metric η. Validate: η^μν η_νρ = δ^μ_ρ, η^μ_μ = 4 (or D in d-reg). Use TensorGR's define_metric\!, set_flat\!.
title: Register Minkowski manifold and η metric in TensorGR registry

close_reason: LorentzIndex kept as Feynfeld-specific struct (architectural decision: BMHV dim tagging at index level doesn't map to TIndex manifold-level dims). Implemented in types.jl with dim::Union{Symbol,Int}
description: Replace current LorentzIndex struct with a thin wrapper or alias around TensorGR's TIndex. A Lorentz index is TIndex(:μ, Up, :Lorentz). Provide convenience constructors matching FeynCalc conventions: 4D, D-dim, (D-4)-dim slots.
title: Define LorentzIndex as TIndex alias with vbundle=:Lorentz

close_reason: BMHV algebra implemented in src/algebra/dimensions.jl: Dim4/DimD/DimDm4 types, 9 projection rules, dim_trace, to_dim. 23 passing tests
description: Implement the BMHV (Breitenlohner-Maison / 't Hooft-Veltman) dimensional projection rules: D∩4→4, D∩(D-4)→(D-4), 4∩(D-4)→0. These rules must fire at construction time (inner constructors). Port from FeynCalc SharedObjects.m Pair rules.
title: BMHV dimension algebra for TIndex

close_reason: Phase 0 validated: 63 tests pass (23 dimension algebra + 12 minkowski registry + 28 type system). Full test suite green
description: Test suite for Phase 0: g^μν g_νρ = δ^μ_ρ, g^μ_μ = D, BMHV projections (4∩D=4, 4∩(D-4)=0, D∩(D-4)=D-4), mixed-dim contractions. Translate relevant FeynCalc MUnit tests from Tests/Lorentz/.
title: Validate Phase 0: metric contraction, dimension algebra, TensorGR integration tests

description: Julia doesn't need FeynCalc's FCI/FCE dual representation — constructors ARE internal form. But provide convenience macros for shorthand input: @sp(p,q) for ScalarProduct, @ga(μ) for DiracGamma, @gs(p) for slashed momentum, @mt(μ,ν) for metric. Pretty-printing for display. Design after Phase 1a types are stable.
title: Cross-cutting: FCI/FCE convenience API (macros/constructors)

description: Port FeynCalc's Dirac algebra: DiracGamma, DOT/DotSimplify, DiracTrick, DiracSimplify, DiracTrace, DiracOrder. This is the hardest part of the algebra layer — 2102 lines of sequence pattern matching in DiracTrick alone.
title: Phase 1b: Dirac Algebra

description: Port FeynCalc DiracGamma. Encodes: γ^μ (DiracGamma[LorentzIndex[μ]]), γ^5, γ^6=(1+γ^5)/2, γ^7=(1-γ^5)/2, slashed momenta (DiracGamma[Momentum[p]]). Dimension rules: DiracGamma[x,4]:=DiracGamma[x], DiracGamma[0,___]:=0, γ^5/6/7 always 4D, 4D object in (D-4) dims = 0. Mark as NonCommutative. Ref: SharedObjects.m DiracGamma section.
title: Implement DiracGamma type with dimension tagging

description: Port FeynCalc DOT = Dot. Non-commutative multiplication for Dirac matrices, SUN generators. DotSimplify: expand NC products, pull out commutative factors, apply user-defined DotSimplifyRelations. In Julia: a DiracChain type (Vector of NC objects). Ref: NonCommAlgebra/DotSimplify.m.
title: Implement DOT (non-commutative product) and DotSimplify

description: THE HARDEST FILE. Port FeynCalc DiracTrick.m (2102 lines). Core rules: γ^μ.γ_μ=D, γ^μ.γ^ν.γ_μ=(2-D)γ^ν, Clifford algebra {γ^μ,γ^ν}=2g^{μν}, commutation of γ^5/6/7 through chains, Dirac equation (p̸-m).u(p)=0, BMHV splitting. Uses sequence pattern matching (x___,γ^5,y___) → in Julia: explicit position-based iteration over chain elements. This will need multiple research rounds.
title: Implement DiracTrick (core Dirac simplification rules)

description: Port FeynCalc DiracSimplify.m (572 lines). Orchestrator that calls: FCDiracIsolate → DiracTrick → Contract → DiracEquation → SpinorChainTrick → SirlinSimplify → DiracOrder. The 'isolate and simplify' pattern: wrap Dirac structures in temp head, process each, reassemble. Ref: Dirac/DiracSimplify.m.
title: Implement DiracSimplify (master Dirac simplifier)

description: Port FeynCalc DiracTrace.m (775 lines). Key: trace is a HEAD that wraps expressions, evaluated only when DiracTraceEvaluate→True. Without γ^5: standard recursive formula. With γ^5: scheme-dependent (NDR, BMHV, Larin). CRITICAL: Tr with single γ^5 in NDR is LEFT UNEVALUATED (genuine physical ambiguity). Must support 4 γ^5 schemes. TraceOfOne default=4. Ref: Dirac/DiracTrace.m.
title: Implement DiracTrace (trace evaluation)

description: Port FeynCalc DiracOrder.m (259 lines). Lexicographic ordering of γ matrices using anticommutation: γ^a.γ^b = 2g^{ab} - γ^b.γ^a. Bubble sort. Custom order list support. Uses FCDiracIsolate. Ref: Dirac/DiracOrder.m.
title: Implement DiracOrder (normal ordering)

description: Port FeynCalc Spinor, SpinorU, SpinorV + DiracEquation (applies Dirac eq for on-shell spinors: (p̸-m)u(p)=0, ū(p)(p̸-m)=0). Also SpinorChainTrick (simplifications of spinor bilinears). Ref: Shared/Spinor.m, Dirac/DiracEquation.m, Dirac/SpinorChainTrick.m.
title: Implement Spinor types and DiracEquation

description: Port FeynCalc FCSetDiracGammaScheme. In FeynCalc: global var DiracGammaScheme. In Julia: use a context/config object passed to trace functions (thread-safe, testable). Support NDR, NDR-Discard, BMHV, Larin. Each scheme affects DiracTrace and DiracTrick behavior. Ref: FCMain.m scheme management.
title: Implement DiracGamma scheme system (NDR/BMHV/Larin)

description: Translate FeynCalc Tests/Dirac/*.test (33 test files). Priority: DiracSimplify.test, DiracTrace.test, DiracTrick.test, DiracOrder.test, DiracEquation.test, SpinorChainTrick.test. These contain the most coverage. Preserve math exactly.
title: Validate Phase 1b: translate Dirac MUnit tests

description: Port FeynRules' Lagrangian DSL, model definition system, and vertex extraction via second-quantization operator algebra. MUST follow reference implementation faithfully — no functional differentiation shortcuts. Core path ~7600 lines.
title: Phase 3: Model + Rules (FeynRules port)

description: Research spike: how to translate FeynRules' M$ variable convention and Mathematica expression DSL (FS[], DC[], Ga[], del[], Dot) to Julia. Options: Julia macros, plain function calls, custom string macros. Must support: gauge kinetic terms, fermion kinetic terms, Yukawa couplings, scalar potential. Study FeynRules Core/ClassDeclarations.m, Models/SM.fr. Deliverable: design document.
title: Design Julia Lagrangian DSL

description: Port FeynRules field declarations. RScalarField, CScalarField, DiracField, MajoranaField, VectorField, GhostField, Spin2Field. Each carries: SelfConjugate, Indices, Mass, QuantumNumbers, PropagatorType. Physical vs Unphysical distinction via Definitions replacement rules. Ref: Core/FieldDeclarations.m (929 lines), Core/ClassDeclarations.m.
title: Implement field type system (scalar, fermion, vector, ghost, tensor)

description: Port FeynRules gauge group handling. How SU(N) groups are defined, generators computed, structure constants derived, gauge fixing works, ghosts generated. M$GaugeGroups declarations. Ref: Core/ClassDeclarations.m gauge group section, Core/Initialisation.m.
title: Implement gauge group system (SU(N) definitions, generators, structure constants)

description: Port FeynRules parameter handling. M$Parameters with internal/external distinction. NumericalValue evaluation. Parameter dependencies and derived quantities. Running couplings. Ref: Core/ClassDeclarations.m parameters, Core/Initialisation.m NumericalValue.
title: Implement parameter system (internal/external, numerical eval)

description: Port FeynRules index system. Index[Lorentz,mu], Index[Colour,i], etc. IndexRange, IndexStyle, IndexDelta, ME (metric). Flavor expansion (class → members with index values). Automatic contraction. Must integrate with TensorGR TIndex for Lorentz indices. Ref: Core/Initialisation.m, Core/PutIndices.m.
title: Implement index engine (typed indices, IndexRange, IndexDelta, contraction)

description: Port FeynRules vertex extraction FAITHFULLY using second-quantization operator algebra. OperatorChain with creation operators appended for each external particle. Fields commuted past creation operators using Commutator. Each commutation yields Kronecker delta × wave function. After normal-ordering, wave functions → 1, giving vertex coupling × i. Derivatives → momentum factors via delCom. MUST follow reference implementation. Ref: Core/ExtractVertexTools.m (373 lines — THE CORE), Core/VertexRoutine.m (776 lines).
title: Implement vertex extraction via operator algebra (THE CORE)

description: Port FeynRules optimized flavor expansion (class→member index summation) and vertex list sorting/merging/fermion chain extraction. Ref: Core/OptimizedFlavExp.m (1328 lines), Core/VertexListHandling.m (1115 lines).
title: Implement flavor expansion and vertex list handling

description: Translate the FeynRules SM model to Julia. All SM fields, parameters, gauge groups, and the full SM Lagrangian. Validate: extract all SM vertices and compare to published Feynman rules. Ref: FeynRules Models/SM/ directory.
title: Implement SM model file

description: Extract all SM 3-point and 4-point vertices from the SM Lagrangian and compare against Peskin & Schroeder Appendix / Romao-Silva SM Feynman rules reference. This is the integration test for the entire Model+Rules layer.
title: Validate Phase 3: SM vertex extraction matches published Feynman rules

description: Port FeynCalc's SU(N) colour algebra: SUNT, SUNTF, SUNF, SUND, SUNDelta, SUNSimplify, SUNTrace, CalcColorFactor. Ref: SUN/ directory (~1657 lines).
title: Phase 1c: SU(N) Colour Algebra

description: Port FeynCalc colour types. SUNT[a] = generator T^a (NonCommutative, lives in DOT chains). SUNTF[{a},i,j] = T^a with explicit fundamental indices. SUNF[a,b,c] = structure constants f^{abc}. SUND[a,b,c] = symmetric d^{abc} (Orderless). SUNDelta[a,b] = δ^{ab} adjoint. SUNFDelta[a,b] = δ^{ab} fundamental. SUNIndex[a] vs SUNFIndex[a]. CA=N, CF=(N²-1)/(2N). Ref: SUN/ types in SharedObjects.m.
title: Implement SU(N) type system (SUNT, SUNF, SUND, SUNDelta, indices)

description: Port FeynCalc SUNSimplify. Key identities: T^a.T^b = δ^{ab}/(2N) + (d^{abc}+if^{abc})T^c/2, f^{acd}f^{bcd}=Nδ^{ab}, δ^{aa}=N²-1, Tr(T^aT^b)=δ^{ab}/2. SUNN↔CA/CF conversions. Ref: SUN/SUNSimplify.m.
title: Implement SUNSimplify (colour algebra simplification)

description: Port FeynCalc SUNTrace. Wraps in SUNTrace head (unevaluated by default). Uses FCColorIsolate to extract colour structures. Traces with 2 or 3 generators auto-evaluated. Explicit mode expresses structure constants as traces. Ref: SUN/SUNTrace.m.
title: Implement SUNTrace (colour trace evaluation)

description: Translate FeynCalc Tests/SUN/*.test (5 test files). Also check Tests/NonCommAlgebra/ for DOT-related colour tests.
title: Validate Phase 1c: translate SUN MUnit tests

description: Design the master simplify() function that orchestrates: ExpandScalarProduct → Contract → DiracSimplify → SUNSimplify → Collect. FeynCalc uses the 'isolate and simplify' pattern: FCDiracIsolate wraps Dirac structures in temp head, process each, reassemble. Need Julia equivalent. Ref: the isolate pattern recurs in DiracSimplify, SUNSimplify, TID.
title: Cross-cutting: expression simplify() pipeline
